architecture.summary: RCE modifies a pretrained LLM's internal representation geometry during inference by spawning dynamically generated low-rank concept subspaces when representational inadequacy is detected. Subspaces are selected via minimum description length criterion, merged when synergistic, and consolidated through constrained optimization. The system reads and writes the model's own hidden states to construct new abstractions in latent space rather than recombining existing ones.

biblio.title: Recursive Concept Evolution for Compositional Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: RCE yields 12-18 point gains on ARC-AGI-2
RCE yields 8-14 point improvements on GPQA and BBH
RCE produces consistent reductions in depth-induced error on MATH and HLE

Existing methods improve reasoning by expanding token-level search through chain-of-thought prompting, self-consistency, or reinforcement learning, but they leave the model's latent representation space fixed
When the required abstraction is not already encoded in the model's latent representation space, performance collapses on compositional reasoning benchmarks
RCE allows the model to construct new abstractions rather than recombining existing ones through dynamically generated low-rank concept subspaces spawned when representational inadequacy is detected
